rules.title: A declaration shall declare an identifier
Composite expression shall not be cast
A declaration shall contain no more than one declarator
All declarations of an object or function shall use the same type and type qualifiers
Function shall have prototype
A character literal shall not be assigned to an object unless the object is const-qualified
goto shall not jump into a block
A u suffix shall be applied to all octal or hexadecimal constants that are represented in an unsigned form
Function pointer conversions shall not be performed
Full objects shall not be read unless initialized
Objects and functions with internal linkage shall be declared static
A file-scope object should be declared in block scope if it is only used by one function
Objects and functions shall not have external linkage unless required
An array shall not be declared without a size
Unique goto labels
Object value shall not be read before being set
No recursion
goto shall not be used
A file-scope function should be reviewed if it is only used by one function
Function declarations shall specify parameter names for all parameters
Switch controlling expression
sizeof operand shall have no side effects
Composite operands shall use compatible types
Wide-character literals shall not be used
Nested switch depth
Logical RHS shall not contain persistent side effects
Composite expressions shall not widen assignments
A string literal shall not be assigned to an object unless the object is const-qualified
Multi-character literals shall not be used
Switch should contain multiple cases
Assignment result shall not be used
Cast shall preserve essential type
The lowercase character l shall not be used in a literal suffix
Switch statement should have default
Increment/decrement expressions shall not contain additional side effects
Binary operators shall use compatible types
Octal constants shall not be used
Switch clauses shall terminate with break
No undefined evaluation order
Assignment shall preserve essential type
Objects shall be initialized before use
Identifier declarations and definitions shall be unique within a scope
Switch statement shall be well formed
Conversions from void pointers shall not be performed
Character types shall not be used in arithmetic expressions
Compound statements required
Pointer and integer conversions should be avoided
Operands shall use appropriate essential types
There shall be no more than one declaration of an identifier in a translation unit
Single point of exit
Compatible declaration shall be visible for externally linked functions
Object pointer casts shall preserve type
Conversions involving incomplete pointer types shall not be performed
The register storage class specifier shall not be used
if-else chain shall terminate with else
Pointer arithmetic shall remain within the same array
Pointer subtraction shall use the same array
Pointer relational comparison shall remain within one array
Pointer arithmetic operators should not be used
Arrays should have explicit size
#include placement
Macro names shall be unique
Balanced conditional preprocessing
Empty macro replacement list
#undef shall not be used

violations.title: All declarations of an object or function shall use the same type and type qualifiers
violations.description: All declarations of an object or function shall use the same type and type qualifiers.
violations.suggested_code: Review these declarations and ensure the same type and qualifiers are used for this identifier.
violations.explanation: Identifier 'x' is declared with inconsistent type information. All declarations of an object or function should use the same type and type qualifiers.

metadata.rationale: Consistent declarations make interfaces easier to understand and reduce the risk of accidental type mismatches.

violations.title: There shall be no more than one declaration of an identifier in a translation unit
violations.description: There shall be no more than one declaration of an identifier in a translation unit.
violations.suggested_code: Review this declaration and ensure the identifier is not declared more than once.
violations.explanation: Identifier 'x' is declared more than once in the translation unit. There should be one and only one declaration for an identifier with external linkage.

metadata.rationale: Repeated declarations make dependencies harder to reason about and can hide accidental linkage mistakes.

violations.title: Compatible declaration shall be visible for externally linked functions
violations.description: Functions with external linkage should have a compatible declaration visible before the definition.
violations.suggested_code: Provide a compatible prototype before the function definition.
violations.explanation: Function 'main' has external linkage but no visible declaration.

metadata.rationale: Ensures consistent interfaces across translation units.

violations.title: Assignment shall preserve essential type
violations.description: Assignments shall not convert between incompatible essential type categories.
violations.explanation: Assignment converts between different essential types.

metadata.rationale: Implicit conversions may lose information.

violations.title: Cast shall preserve essential type

metadata.rationale: Unnecessary casts hide programming errors.

violations.title: Objects and functions shall not have external linkage unless required

violations.suggested_code: Review whether this file-scope declaration should be restricted to internal linkage.

metadata.rationale: Reducing external linkage minimizes global coupling and improves encapsulation.

violations.title: A file-scope function should be reviewed if it is only used by one function
violations.description: A file-scope function that is only used by one function should be reviewed for scope reduction.
